steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The assertion claims Henry was motivated by “perceived financial opportunities.” To support this, we need evidence showing Henry assessed the financial worth of monastery properties before seizing them.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C reveals that Henry commissioned a survey in January 1535 to determine the value of church holdings, right before the Dissolution began—direct evidence he perceived financial opportunities in these properties.

Choices A, B, and D describe demographics, what happened to monastery contents, and social impacts respectively—none address Henry's financial motivation.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
